description: Self-write idempotence lint (§51.0.F.1). Inside <Loading rule=.Done>, the write @phase = .Loading re-asserts the current variant. Statically detectable (literal .Variant matching the enclosing state-child), so the compile-time info lint W-ENGINE-SELF-WRITE-DETECTED fires. Critically, .Loading is NOT in .Loading's rule= set (.Done), yet NO E-ENGINE-INVALID-TRANSITION fires — a self-write is reclassified as a runtime no-op, NOT a rule= violation (the trichotomy's no-op arm).
rationale: §51.0.F.1 a self-write to the current variant is a runtime no-op (NOT a rule= violation) regardless of whether rule= lists the current variant; the compile-time W-ENGINE-SELF-WRITE-DETECTED info lint surfaces it.
